hotspots.title: Poland’s constitutional overhaul push
hotspots.blurb: President Karol Nawrocki has launched a council to draft a new constitution by 2030, sparking intense political debate. The move is framed as necessary to modernize Poland’s governance but is criticized as a power grab by opposition parties. Multiple articles highlight the council’s composition, including controversial figures like Julia Przyłębska, and the polarized reactions from political leaders.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its high-stakes framing as a defining moment for Poland’s political future, with moral panic over democratic backsliding and cross-party contention.

hotspots.title: Magyar Péter’s nepotism and government formation
hotspots.blurb: Hungary’s incoming Prime Minister Péter Magyar has faced backlash for appointing his brother-in-law as justice minister, sparking accusations of nepotism. The controversy is amplified by Magyar’s criticism of previous governments for similar practices, creating a narrative of hypocrisy. Articles also highlight internal party tensions and public outrage over the appointment’s implications for judicial independence.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to the moral outrage over perceived hypocrisy and the high affective investment in Hungary’s political transition, with polarized framing and accusations of systemic corruption.

hotspots.title: Trump administration’s Iran war costs and strategies
hotspots.blurb: The Trump administration’s handling of the Iran war continues to dominate coverage, with recent developments including the rejection of a new Iranian peace proposal and the announcement of troop withdrawals from Germany. Articles highlight the economic and geopolitical consequences, such as rising oil prices and strained NATO relations, while framing the administration’s actions as reckless and divisive.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to the high affective investment in war rhetoric, economic alarmism, and polarized framing of Trump’s foreign policy as a defining issue for U.S. global standing.

hotspots.title: Vox’s ‘national priority’ and regional pacts in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Vox’s ‘national priority’ policy, which prioritizes Spaniards over immigrants in housing, social services, and public aid, has sparked nationwide backlash. The PSOE has launched institutional offensives to block the policy, calling it discriminatory and unconstitutional. Articles highlight the policy’s role in regional coalition deals with the PP and its polarizing impact on Spain’s political landscape.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to the moral outrage over perceived discrimination, polarized contention between parties, and the policy’s role in shaping regional governance.

hotspots.title: Norway’s FrP racism scandal and internal fallout
hotspots.blurb: Norway’s Progress Party (FrP) has faced intense scrutiny after a senior advisor’s racist remarks about Pakistanis were exposed. Party leader Sylvi Listhaug condemned the remarks but faced criticism for her handling of the scandal. Articles highlight the internal disciplinary actions, public backlash, and the broader implications for FrP’s reputation and electoral prospects.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to the moral outrage over racist rhetoric, polarized framing of the party’s response, and the scandal’s potential to reshape Norway’s political landscape.
